entry.title: Gold Eagle Clearinghouse Targets Security Gap, But How Is Unclear
entry.content:
The White House launched Gold Eagle to coordinate vulnerability response in a new AI world, but multiple questions linger over how it's being implemented.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: The Real AI Threat Is Blind Trust
entry.content:
AI models left to both interpret and execute commands eliminate critical cybersecurity oversight.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Inc Ransomware Exploits SonicWall SMA Zero-Days
entry.content:
When chained together, the two vulnerabilities allow threat actors to gain root-level capabilities on SonicWall's mobile access appliances.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Cybersecurity Keeps Events 'Uneventful'
entry.content:
From the World Cup to the United States' 250th celebration, this year's event calendar has been packed with high-profile gatherings that drew global audiences, intense scrutiny, and enormous security demands.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Attackers Combo Up Evasion Tactics for BEC Phishing
entry.content:
"The TFF Trap" uses fileless techniques and loaders with low detection rates to deploy various RATs and stealers, including Agent Tesla, Remcos, XWorm, and Best Private Logger.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: 25 Years After Code Red: What the Worm Era Can Teach Us About AI Security
entry.content:
Marc Maiffret reflects on Code Red's legacy and the security lessons helping organizations navigate AI risk today.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Hacker Turns AI Jailbreaks Into Offensive Attack Platform
entry.content:
A Russian-speaking actor, "Trim," dismantled publicly available frontier models and integrated them with offensive security tools.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Ransomware Is Accelerating, But It's Not Because of AI
entry.content:
Researchers pointed to fragmentation of the ransomware ecosystem, the emergence of new attackers, and expansion of attacks on less defended organizations.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Clover Health Investments Discloses Data Breach
entry.content:
Using social engineering, hackers compromised employee accounts with access to personal and health information. The post Clover Health Investments Discloses Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Meta Paid $78,000 Bounty for Vulnerability Exposing Customer Support Data
entry.content:
A security researcher discovered a broken access control vulnerability in Meta’s support infrastructure. The post Meta Paid $78,000 Bounty for Vulnerability Exposing Customer Support Data appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Estée Lauder Discloses Impact From Oracle EBS Zero-Day Hack
entry.content:
Hackers exfiltrated personal, financial, and health information from the company’s Oracle EBS instance in August 2025. The post Estée Lauder Discloses Impact From Oracle EBS Zero-Day Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: New HollowGraph Malware Abuses Microsoft 365 Calendar for C&C Communication
entry.content:
Part of a larger toolkit, HollowGraph uses a compromised 365 account’s calendar as a two-way dead-drop. The post New HollowGraph Malware Abuses Microsoft 365 Calendar for C&C Communication appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: SecurityWeek Launches Critical Impact Awards to Recognize Excellence in Industrial Cybersecurity
entry.content:
Independently judged and sponsor-neutral, the new awards program honors the people, organizations, and technologies delivering proven impact in industrial cybersecurity; winners to be announced live at the 2026 ICS Cybersecurity Conference in Nashville The post SecurityWeek Launches Critical Impact Awards to Recognize Excellence in Industrial Cybersecurity appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Empirical Security Raises $25 Million in Series A Funding
entry.content:
The startup will use the investment to accelerate the development of its threat prediction and discovery products. The post Empirical Security Raises $25 Million in Series A Funding appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Cisco Launches Low-Cost AI Models for Source Code Security
entry.content:
The open-weight Antares models are designed to pinpoint known vulnerabilities in codebases faster and at a fraction of the cost of larger AI models. The post Cisco Launches Low-Cost AI Models for Source Code Security appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: OpenAI Says Its AI Models Broke Loose and Hacked Hugging Face
entry.content:
The admission comes days after Hugging Face disclosed an attack powered by autonomous AI agents. The post OpenAI Says Its AI Models Broke Loose and Hacked Hugging Face appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Kenya probes hack of president's website after bitcoin ransom demand
entry.content:
The website was hacked on Saturday, when its homepage was replaced with a message displaying a cryptocurrency wallet address and threatening to publish unspecified information about President William Ruto unless the ransom was paid.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Taiwan to slow mobile data during national resilience drills
entry.content:
The speed of 5G and 4G networks across much of Taiwan will be temporarily reduced to 1 percent of capacity as the island holds annual civilian and military exercises.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Spain fines 23andMe nearly $3 million for cybersecurity failings enabling 2023 hack
entry.content:
The Agencia Española de Protección de Datos (AEPD) announced the fine on Friday, saying in its decision that more than 2,600 Spaniards were impacted by a breach affecting 6.9 million people worldwide.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
entry.content:
The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
entry.content:
A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
entry.content:
Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport network in the Greater London area. The duo were key members of a prolific cybercrime group known as Scattered Spider, and their guilty pleas came on the first day of what was expected to be a six-week trial.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Felons, Fraudsters Flog Offensive Cybersecurity Startup
entry.content:
A cybersecurity startup dangling millions of dollars to acquire zero-day security vulnerabilities in popular software is run by a pair of far-right conspiracy theorists and convicted felons whose most recent ventures included fake intelligence companies and a now-defunct AI-based lobbying platform they operated under assumed names.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak
entry.content:
The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued a postmortem on a data leak in which a contractor published dozens of internal CISA credentials -- including AWS Govcloud keys -- in a public GitHub repository for almost six months before being notified by KrebsOnSecurity. Experts say the gaps identified in the agency's initial response provide important lessons that all security teams should absorb.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Microsoft Patches a Record 570 Security Flaws
entry.content:
Microsoft Corp. today released software updates to plug at least 570 security holes in its Windows operating systems and other software, almost triple the number of vulnerabilities the software giant fixed in its record-smashing Patch Tuesday release last month. Microsoft attributed the burgeoning patch counts to vulnerability discoveries aided by artificial intelligence.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Vision Care Providers Settle Data Breach Class Actions
entry.content:
Settlements have been agreed to resolve class action lawsuits against two vision care providers: Total Vision in California and Naper […] The post Vision Care Providers Settle Data Breach Class Actions appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Free Webinar Today: Making Sense of HIPAA Compliance
entry.content:
Join Bradley King, a senior compliance consultant, as he provides a clear explanation for small medical practices on what they […] The post Free Webinar Today: Making Sense of HIPAA Compliance appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: All About Women’s Care Data Breach Affects Up to 12,000 Patients
entry.content:
All About Women’s Care in Colorado has notified 12,000 patients that their data has been compromised in a data breach, […] The post All About Women’s Care Data Breach Affects Up to 12,000 Patients appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Centers Laboratory Discloses Data Breach Affecting 542K Individuals
entry.content:
Centers Lab NJ LLC, a Hanover, New Jersey-based diagnostic testing laboratory that provides medical and diagnostic testing services to healthcare […] The post Centers Laboratory Discloses Data Breach Affecting 542K Individuals appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Abbott Investigating Cyberattack Claims From Two Threat Actors
entry.content:
The healthcare giant Abbott is investigating claims from two threat groups who allege cyberattacks and data theft, one involving legacy […] The post Abbott Investigating Cyberattack Claims From Two Threat Actors appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: 23andMe Pays $18 Million to Settle Multistate Data Breach Lawsuit
entry.content:
A coalition of 42 state attorneys general has agreed to a $18 million settlement with 23andMe (now Chrome Holding Co.) […] The post 23andMe Pays $18 Million to Settle Multistate Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: ApolloMD Agrees to Pay $4.02M to Settle Data Breach Lawsuit
entry.content:
ApolloMD Business Services, a business associate that provides integrated, multispecialty physician, APC, and practice management services, has agreed to settle […] The post ApolloMD Agrees to Pay $4.02M to Settle Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Major Healthcare Software Vendor Investigating Cyberattack
entry.content:
The healthcare technology company Craneware is investigating a cybersecurity incident and has confirmed that a significant amount of data was […] The post Major Healthcare Software Vendor Investigating Cyberattack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Unknown
entry.content:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Hackers steal $23.7 million in crypto from Ostium in off-chain attack
entry.content:
The Ostium trading platform announced that an attacker stole $23.75 million from its liquidity provider vault last week, after compromising off-chain infrastructure used to feed prices into the protocol. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Estée Lauder discloses data breach via Oracle E-Business flaw
entry.content:
Cosmetics giant Estée Lauder is notifying employees of a data breach after hackers exploited a flaw in Oracle E-Business Suite that the company used for human resources (HR) operations. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Critical Palo Alto VPN bug now exploited by Qilin ransomware gang
entry.content:
The Qilin ransomware gang is exploiting a critical PAN-OS GlobalProtect authentication bypass flaw to breach victims' networks, according to cybersecurity company Arctic Wolf. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Anubis ransomware claims Coca-Cola Fairlife attack, threatens data leak
entry.content:
The Anubis ransomware gang has claimed responsibility for the cyberattack on Coca-Cola's Fairlife dairy subsidiary, threatening to publish allegedly stolen corporate data unless the company pays a ransom. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: OpenAI says its AI models hacked Hugging Face during testing
entry.content:
OpenAI says its AI models, including GPT‑5.6 Sol and a pre-release model, hacked into the Hugging Face artificial intelligence repository while being tested in a sandboxed testing environment. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Chick-fil-A discloses data breach after credential stuffing attacks
entry.content:
American fast food restaurant chain Chick-fil-A is notifying customers of a data breach after their accounts were hacked in a wave of recent credential stuffing attacks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: NY Attorney General James Secures $18 Million From 23andMe for Failing to Protect Customers’ Genetic Data
entry.content:
There’s another update in the litigation involving 23andMe, below, but this won’t be the last update, as California’s Attorney General has also recently sued them under California’s privacy laws. New York Attorney General Letitia James and a bipartisan coalition of 42 other attorneys general today secured $18 million from genetic testing company 23andMe for failing to... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Medical giant Abbott investigates two cyber incidents as ShinyHunters and ShadowByt3$ both claim breaches
entry.content:
Anna Zhadan reports: American healthcare giant Abbott Laboratories is investigating two cyber incidents that appear to be unrelated: one involving its Cancer Diagnostics business and another affecting its LabCentral portal. Although unrelated, the two disclosures came within days of each other. On July 16th, Abbott said it was investigating an incident involving unauthorized access to a limited... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Broken Promises of Anonymity: Four Months Later, Still No Transparency. Now We’re Seeking Accountability.
entry.content:
On March 18, 2026, Navigate360 learned that 8.3 million anonymous tips had been exposed. The company’s response—and the silence of the programs that depend on its platform—has persisted for months. We’re now seeking accountability through state and federal regulators. A DataBreaches.net Editorial In March 2026, the world learned that a hacktivist had acquired 8.3 million... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Seoul Notifies 4.62 Million of Ttareungyi Data Breach, Offers Free Passes
entry.content:
Kim Eun-bi reports: The Seoul Metropolitan Government will send individual text messages to about 4.62 million citizens affected by a data breach involving membership information for Ttareungyi, the city’s public bike-sharing service, notifying them of the leaked items and offering compensation such as 30-day passes. The Seoul Facilities Corporation said the same day that it... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Suno Data Breach had a breach in 2025. Why is it first being known now?
entry.content:
Millions here, tens of millions there. Are we all getting breach fatigue by now? Over on HaveIBeenPwned, Troy Hunt reports that Suno experienced a data breach in November 2025, which 404 Media first made public this month: In November 2025, AI music generation tool Suno suffered a data breach that later came to light in July the... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: NYSDFS Secures $50 Million Penalty from Swedbank for Withholding Information from Investigators
entry.content:
One of the biggest breaches of 2016 was the Panama Papers leak. The law firm at the heart of it, Mossack Fonseca, closed its doors in 2018, unable to recover from all the damage. But while the law firm folded, investigations continued. The New York Department of Financial Services announced that it has settled charges... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Personal data of all South Korean diplomats believed leaked in ‘unprecedented’ cyberattack
entry.content:
Seo Ji-Eun reports: The personal information of nearly all of South Korea’s diplomatic personnel is presumed to have been compromised in what the Foreign Ministry on Tuesday called an “unprecedented” cyberattack, exposing up to 10,000 administrative and intelligence records. The data system of the Korea National Diplomatic Academy (KNDA) — an institution affiliated with the... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Cyberattack against Maine telecom disrupted municipal internet service in 23 towns
entry.content:
Colin Wood reports: At least one municipal government was among those to see their internet service disrupted after a cyberattack against a Maine telecommunications firm Sunday caused an outage affecting 23 towns along the state’s midcoastal region. A local NBC affiliate reported that the government office of Damariscotta, a tourist town of roughly 2,300 inhabitants, known for... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Pay up or not? Ransomware surge has victims facing tough choices.
entry.content:
Hannah Murphy reports: Nearly half of companies that are targets of a ransomware cyber attack end up paying a ransom to release their data or systems, according to 2025 research from cybersecurity group Sophos, while the median amount demanded is rising. Globally, some jurisdictions are responding by banning payments to hackers. In the UK, for... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Milford, New Hampshire Confirms Unauthorized Activity, Withholds Details of Suspected Cyberattack
entry.content:
Milford, New Hampshire is a quintessential New England town. But charm is no defense against cyberattackers, and it appears that the town may have been attacked last week. As DysruptionHub was the first to report, the town began experiencing problems early on July 15. Town email alerts, shared with DataBreaches by a town resident, reveal... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.content:

entry.title: BAYADA Home Health Care, Inc.
entry.content:
